Metadata.title: Ibrik Kitchen — Balkan Kitchen, Paris Sentier
Metadata.description: A Balkan kitchen in the Sentier, Paris 2. Romanian, Greek, Serbian and Turkish classics meet French bistronomy. The Balkans are home to everyone.

Hero.blurb: A small, loud, warm Balkan kitchen in the Sentier. Slow food from the Carpathians to the Aegean, from somebody's bunică's back garden — cooked the way it has always been cooked.

About.title:
A kitchen
remembered
by hand.
About.p1: Ibrik began with Ecaterina — a tax lawyer from Bucharest who arrived in Paris at six, then quit law to cook the food she'd missed. Ibrik Café opened in 2018; Ibrik Kitchen, in the heart of the Sentier, followed in 2019.
About.p2: Her recipes travel from the Carpathians to the Aegean — sarmale, mititei and cevapcici, souvlaki, halloumi, papanași — cooked by award-winning chefs with one quiet idea: the Balkans are home to everyone.

Menu.lead: Six starters, six mains, four sweets, and the drinks our grandmothers warned us about. Order three plates between two. Stay for coffee.
Menu.foot: Prices in € · 12% serviciu inclus pentru grupuri de 6+

items.salata_vinete: Charred eggplant whipped with sunflower oil & onion, served with sourdough toast.
items.zacusca: Roasted peppers, eggplant & tomato slow-cooked for hours. A taste of late summer.
items.mititei: Skinless grilled minced beef & lamb sausages with mustard and pickles.
items.branza_ardei: Aged sheep's milk cheese, hot peppers, cured red onion, walnut bread.
items.ciorba_burta: Garlic-laced cream broth with vinegar & hot pepper on the side. Cure for everything.
items.ciorba_radauteana: Bukovinian chicken sour soup, sour cream, lemon, garlic oil.
items.sarmale: Pickled cabbage parcels of pork, beef & rice, simmered with smoked bacon. Served on a bed of mămăligă with sour cream.
items.mamaliga: Stone-ground polenta, sheep's cheese, soft butter, a fried duck egg.
items.tochitura: Pork shoulder & smoked sausage stew, polenta, fried egg, telemea cheese.
items.muschi_vita: Dry-aged ribeye over beech embers, marrow butter, charred leeks, pickled cherry.
items.crap: Whole Danube carp with garlic, fresh thyme & roasted potatoes. Sour cream sauce.
items.ardei_umpluti: Sweet peppers stuffed with rice & herbs, tomato broth, dill, smetana.
items.papanasi: Fried sweet cheese dumplings, sour cherry compote, thick sour cream. The reason you came.
items.cozonac: Slow-proofed walnut & cocoa swirl bread, warmed, with mahogany butter.
items.cremsnit: Layered crisp pastry, vanilla custard, snow of icing sugar.
items.placinta_mere: Lard-pastry coil filled with cinnamon apples, cinnamon ice cream.
items.ibrik_coffee: Slow-brewed in copper over hot sand. Sweet, bitter, cardamom. The house namesake.
items.tuica: Twice-distilled plum eau-de-vie, 52°. Served the proper way — cold, a thimble, before the meal.
items.visinata: House-infused sour cherries, sugar, time. Dangerously easy to drink.
items.socata: Wild elderflower & lemon, fermented two days. Non-alcoholic.
items.feteasca: Dealu Mare, 2021. Dark fruit, smoked plum, a long quiet finish.
items.grasa: Cotnari, 2022. Honeyed, dry, with a whisper of acacia.

Press.title:
They keep
coming back.
Press.q1: The sarmale is a love letter written in cabbage and smoke. We did not stop eating.
Press.q2: An act of memory. Ibrik makes a Bucharest grandmother's table feel inevitable, even necessary.
Press.q3: A papanași so tender it briefly cured a hangover and a heartbreak in the same bite.

Events.p1: There is a Romanian word — petrecere — that means a party, a feast, and the act of "passing time" all at once. We were built for it.
Events.p2: Whether it's twelve around the long table or sixty taking the whole room, we cook the way our families cook for theirs: too much, all at once, and so good that nobody quite leaves on time.
Events.card1Meta: 12–24 guests · whole back room · 3 courses, family style
Events.card2Meta: Up to 60 guests · full restaurant · custom menu with the kitchen
Events.card3Meta: Catering off-site · sarmale by the hundred · țuică by the case
Events.card4Meta: Saturdays · 4 hours · 8 hands at the counter · papanași included

Reserve.lead: We hold a few tables back for walk-ins each night, but the room fills early on weekends. Book ahead — we'll keep the candle lit.
Reserve.confirmed_one: Your request for {count} person at {time} is in. We'll confirm by email within the hour. Pe curând.
Reserve.confirmed_other: Your request for {count} people at {time} is in. We'll confirm by email within the hour. Pe curând.
Reserve.name: Your name · numele
Reserve.notes: Notes · note · allergies, occasions, anything we should know
Reserve.notesPh: Birthday for Andrei — please no candles
Reserve.asideP1: Two seats are always kept at the counter for the brave and the hungry — first come, served immediately, with a țuică on the house.
Reserve.asideP2: For larger parties or last-minute miracles, call us.

Location.addressLines:
9 rue de Mulhouse
75002 Paris · Sentier
France

MenusPage.metaDescription: Lunch, dinner and Saturday lunch menus at Ibrik Kitchen, 9 rue de Mulhouse, Paris 2. Spring 2026 — Balkan cuisine, mezze, mains, sweets and drinks.
MenusPage.title:
Three menus,
one kitchen.
MenusPage.lead: Weekday lunch with a quick mezze + main formula, a longer dinner that lingers, and a slower Saturday lunch with sarmale and the patronne's seven-hour lamb if you ask in time.
MenusPage.priceNote: Prices in € · VAT included · service not included
